version.approval_notes:
OpenPath Firefox extension review notes

Purpose
- Browser companion for managed OpenPath devices in education environments.
- Explains blocked pages/resources, supports access requests when enabled, and checks local allowlist state through the OpenPath native host.

Permission rationale
- <all_urls>: page activity and page-resource observers detect blocked third-party resources regardless of origin.
- webRequest/webRequestBlocking: detect and apply managed blocked navigation/resource decisions.
- webNavigation: clears per-tab state when navigation changes.
- tabs: scopes badge and popup data to the active tab.
- nativeMessaging: talks only to the local OpenPath native host installed on the same managed computer.
- storage: keeps managed config and local runtime state in browser storage.
- clipboardWrite: copies blocked-domain lists only after a user action.

Network/data behavior
- Auto-allow observes fetch, XMLHttpRequest, and dynamic resource insertions.
- It sends only candidate host/URL, origin page, resource type, and policy reason to the configured OpenPath service.
- Firefox loads the MAIN-world page-resource observer via manifest; the page-activity content script can also inject the same observer as a page script for Firefox-version coverage.
- No third-party analytics, profiling, advertising, or sale/sharing of browser data is included.

Google games guard
- Limited to supported Google Search and Doodles domains.
- Applies school policy to Google Search/Doodles games only.

Source review
- Human-readable source is attached with the submission.
- See SOURCE_REVIEW_NOTES.md in that archive for rebuild and review notes.

release_notes.en-US:
OpenPath Firefox companion build for managed education devices.

This version includes the blocked-page/request flow, managed page-resource auto-allow observation for classroom resources, local OpenPath native-host integration, and the Google Search/Doodles games policy guard. It does not include third-party analytics or telemetry.
